prd.vision: Create a real-time contest monitoring dashboard for organizers to manage and track competitive programming contests with dynamic state updates, complex filtering, and instant leaderboard recalculation.

userPersonas.persona: Contest Organizers (1-5 people managing the event)

feature.name: Contest Overview Dashboard
feature.description: Display contest health metrics at a glance

components.component: Contest Countdown Timer (real-time, updates every second)
Recent Activities Feed (reverse chronological, last 15 events)

feature.name: Participant Management Table
feature.description: Manage and monitor all contest participants

functionality.item: Sort by rank, name, or penalty time
Multi-level filtering: rank range, institution, problems solved count

feature.name: Submission Monitoring System
feature.description: Track and filter all contest submissions

functionality.item: Filter by verdict (dropdown: All, AC, WA, TLE, RE)

feature.name: Dynamic Leaderboard
feature.description: Real-time ranking display with automatic updates

rankingLogic.secondary: Penalty time (ascending - lower is better, used as tiebreaker)

updateTriggers.trigger: Real-time as submissions come in (if not frozen)

feature.name: Leaderboard Freeze Mode
feature.description: Lock rankings while submissions continue to be received

behavior.item: When frozen: submissions still appear in Submission Monitoring, but leaderboard display is locked
Frozen state snapshot is stored when toggled
When unfrozen: rankings are recalculated with all accumulated submissions
Visual indicator shows when freeze mode is active

feature.name: Rejudge System
feature.description: Manual verdict changes with automatic cascading updates

userFlow.step: Admin clicks on verdict cell in Submission Monitoring
Admin selects new verdict from dropdown

cascade.step: Recalculate rankings (only if leaderboard NOT frozen)
Log activity: "Submission rejudged from X to Y"
UI updates reflect all changes instantly

feature.name: Activity Feed
feature.description: System event log in reverse chronological order

display.item: Each event: timestamp, description, optional metadata
Icon indicators for different event types

implementation.item: Saved to localStorage, persists across refreshes
Exports: Rank, Name, Institution, Problems Solved, Penalty Time

feature.name: Responsive Design
feature.description: Mobile-first layout that works on all screen sizes

breakpoint.behavior: Stack components vertically, sidebar becomes hamburger menu, tables become cards
Full layout visible, all columns in tables, fixed sidebar

functional.requirement: Multi-level filtering with AND logic (all filters must pass)
Debounced search (300ms delay to prevent lag)

technical.requirement: Mock data layer (extensible to real API)

successCriteria.criterion: All 8 MUST features working without bugs
Responsive design tested on 3+ screen sizes
Deployed on Vercel with live URL

evaluationCriteria.criterion: State Management (Zustand store design, data flow)
Scalability (can grow to real backend, extensible)

architecture.overview:
The application follows a strict separation of concerns pattern with 4 distinct layers: 1. Presentational Components (UI only, no logic) 2. Zustand Stores (state management, actions) 3. Business Logic Engines (pure functions) 4. Mock Data Layer (decoupled, testable)
This ensures maintainability, testability, and scalability.

actions.action: Update contest status (upcoming→live→ended)
Decrement countdown by 1 second (called every 1000ms)
Update any metric (participants, submissions, etc)

state.property: Current filter state (search, institution, rank range)

actions.action: Sort participants by problems (desc) then penalty (asc), update ranks
Apply multi-level filters and reset to page 1
Sort current filtered list by field
Navigate to pagination page
Add/subtract problems solved for a participant
Change verdict of a submission (triggers cascades)
Filter by verdict (Pending, AC, WA, TLE, RE, etc)
Filter by problem ID
Filter by participant name
Search by participant name (debounced in component)
Add new submission and log activity
Switch theme and save to localStorage
Change active page
Toggle sidebar (mobile)
Toggle freeze mode, store snapshot if freezing
Add new activity to front of array, keep max 100
Clear all activities

engine.name: rankingEngine
engine.purpose: Calculate participant rankings based on problems solved and penalty time
engine.algorithm:
1. Sort participants by: - Primary: Problems solved (descending, higher is better rank) - Secondary: Penalty time (ascending, lower is better, used as tiebreaker) 2. Assign rank = array index + 1 3. Return sorted array with updated currentRank property
Example: - Participant A: 8 problems, 245 min penalty → Rank 1 - Participant B: 8 problems, 260 min penalty → Rank 2 (same problems, higher penalty) - Participant C: 7 problems, 200 min penalty → Rank 3 (fewer problems)

calledWhen.trigger: Submission received with Accepted verdict (if not frozen)

engine.name: filterEngine
engine.purpose: Apply multi-level filters to participants and submissions
engine.logic: AND logic - ALL filters must pass for item to be included

participantFilters.filter: Problems solved greater than or equal

engine.name: rejudgeManager
engine.purpose: Handle submission verdict changes with cascading updates
engine.cascade: 1. Update submission verdict in submissionStore 2. Calculate problem count delta (Accepted now vs Accepted before) 3. Update participant score: problemsSolved += delta 4. If NOT frozen: recalculateRankings() 5. Log activity: "Submission rejudged from X to Y" 6. UI components subscribed to stores re-render automatically
engine.pseudoCode:
handleRejudge(submissionId, newVerdict): oldSubmission = submissionStore.getSubmission(submissionId) oldVerdict = oldSubmission.verdict
submissionStore.updateSubmissionVerdict(submissionId, newVerdict)
isNewlyAccepted = oldVerdict != Accepted && newVerdict == Accepted isNoLongerAccepted = oldVerdict == Accepted && newVerdict != Accepted
problemsDelta = 0 if isNewlyAccepted: problemsDelta = +1 if isNoLongerAccepted: problemsDelta = -1
participantStore.updateParticipantScore(oldSubmission.participantId, problemsDelta)
if NOT uiStore.isFrozen: participantStore.recalculateRankings()
activityStore.logActivity("Submission rejudged", ...)

dataFlow.flow: User clicks on verdict cell in Submission Monitoring table ↓ RejudgeModal opens with current verdict ↓ User selects new verdict and confirms ↓ submissionStore.updateSubmissionVerdict(submissionId, newVerdict) ↓ participantStore.updateParticipantScore(participantId, delta) ↓ IF (NOT isFrozen): participantStore.recalculateRankings() ELSE: (rankings locked until unfreeze) ↓ activityStore.logActivity("Submission rejudged...") ↓ All subscribed UI components re-render: - Submission table shows new verdict - Participant table shows updated problem count - Leaderboard shows updated rank (if not frozen) - Activity feed shows rejudge event

User clicks "Freeze Leaderboard" button ↓ uiStore.toggleFreeze() ↓ Component stores snapshot: frozenSnapshot = [...rankedParticipants] ↓ Leaderboard component displays frozenSnapshot instead of rankedParticipants ↓ New submissions still arrive and are logged: - submissionStore receives submission - participantStore updates scores - BUT: recalculateRankings() is SKIPPED (isFrozen === true) - Activity feed shows new submission ↓ After contest, user clicks "Unfreeze Leaderboard" ↓ uiStore.toggleFreeze() ↓ frozenSnapshot is cleared ↓ participantStore.recalculateRankings() is called ONCE ↓ Leaderboard instantly updates with all accumulated submissions

User types in search box ↓ Component state updates (searchTerm = "priya") ↓ useDebounce hook waits 300ms ↓ After 300ms of no new input: ↓ setFilters({ ...filters, searchTerm: debouncedValue }) ↓ participantStore.setFilters() applies applyParticipantFilters() ↓ Filtered results update in table ↓ User also selects institution filter ↓ setFilters({ ...filters, institution: "VIT" }) ↓ applyParticipantFilters() applies AND logic: Return participants WHERE (name contains "priya") AND (institution == "VIT") ↓ Table shows intersection of both filters

architecture.componentHierarchy: Root Layout ├── Header │ ├── Logo / Contest Name │ ├── ThemeToggle │ └── Navigation Links ├── Sidebar │ ├── Dashboard link │ ├── Participants link │ ├── Submissions link │ └── Leaderboard link └── Main Content Area ├── [Dashboard Page] │ ├── ContestOverview │ │ ├── StatsCard (x4: Participants, Submissions, Accepted, Rejected) │ │ ├── StatusBadge │ │ └── Acceptance Rate │ ├── ContestCountdown │ │ ├── Timer (HH:MM:SS) │ │ └── Real-time update every 1s │ └── ActivityFeed │ ├── ActivityItem (x15, reverse chronological) │ ├── Timestamp │ ├── Event type icon │ └── Description │ ├── [Participants Page] │ ├── FilterPanel │ │ ├── SearchInput (debounced) │ │ ├── InstitutionSelect │ │ ├── RankRangeInputs │ │ └── ProblemasSolvedInput │ ├── ParticipantTable │ │ ├── ParticipantRow (x10 per page) │ │ │ ├── Rank │ │ │ ├── Name (clickable for details) │ │ │ ├── Institution │ │ │ ├── Problems Solved │ │ │ └── Penalty Time │ │ └── Pagination │ │ ├── Previous button │ │ ├── Page indicator │ │ └── Next button │ ├── [Submissions Page] │ ├── FilterBar │ │ ├── VerdictSelect │ │ ├── ProblemSelect │ │ └── ParticipantSearch │ └── SubmissionTable │ ├── SubmissionRow (x all filtered) │ │ ├── Participant Name │ │ ├── Problem Name │ │ ├── Submission Time │ │ ├── VerdictBadge (clickable to rejudge) │ │ └── Language │ └── RejudgeModal (conditional) │ ├── Current Verdict display │ ├── New Verdict dropdown │ ├── Confirm button │ └── Cancel button │ └── [Leaderboard Page] ├── FreezeToggle button ├── FreezeNotice (conditional, if frozen) └── LeaderboardTable └── LeaderboardRow (x50 displayed) ├── Rank (auto-calculated) ├── Participant Name ├── Problems Solved └── Penalty Time
architecture.folderStructure: src/ ├── pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # Dashboard page │ ├── participants.tsx # Participant management │ ├── submissions.tsx # Submission monitoring │ ├── leaderboard.tsx # Leaderboard │ ├── _app.tsx # Next.js app wrapper │ └── _document.tsx # HTML document wrapper │ ├── components/ │ ├── layout/ │ │ ├── Header.tsx │ │ ├── Sidebar.tsx │ │ ├── ThemeToggle.tsx │ │ └── Layout.tsx │ ├── dashboard/ │ │ ├── StatsCard.tsx │ │ ├── ContestOverview.tsx │ │ ├── ContestCountdown.tsx │ │ └── ActivityFeed.tsx │ ├── participants/ │ │ ├── ParticipantTable.tsx │ │ ├── ParticipantRow.tsx │ │ ├── FilterPanel.tsx │ │ └── Pagination.tsx │ ├── submissions/ │ │ ├── SubmissionTable.tsx │ │ ├── SubmissionRow.tsx │ │ ├── VerdictBadge.tsx │ │ ├── SubmissionFilters.tsx │ │ └── RejudgeModal.tsx │ ├── leaderboard/ │ │ ├── LeaderboardTable.tsx │ │ ├── LeaderboardRow.tsx │ │ ├── FreezeToggle.tsx │ │ └── RankIndicator.tsx │ └── common/ │ ├── Badge.tsx │ ├── Modal.tsx │ ├── Spinner.tsx │ └── ErrorBoundary.tsx │ ├── stores/ │ ├── contestStore.ts │ ├── participantStore.ts │ ├── submissionStore.ts │ ├── uiStore.ts │ ├── activityStore.ts │ └── index.ts │ ├── lib/ │ ├── engines/ │ │ ├── rankingEngine.ts │ │ ├── filterEngine.ts │ │ └── rejudgeManager.ts │ ├── utils/ │ │ ├── formatters.ts │ │ ├── validators.ts │ │ └── csvExporter.ts │ └── types/ │ ├── contest.ts │ ├── participant.ts │ ├── submission.ts │ └── activity.ts │ ├── data/ │ ├── mock/ │ │ ├── mockParticipants.ts (150 entries) │ │ ├── mockSubmissions.ts (500+ entries) │ │ ├── mockProblems.ts (10-15 entries) │ │ ├── mockActivities.ts (seeded) │ │ └── mockContest.ts │ └── seeds/ │ └── generateMockData.ts │ ├── styles/ │ ├── globals.css │ ├── responsive.css │ └── [Component].module.css │ ├── hooks/ │ ├── useContestTimer.ts │ ├── useDebounce.ts │ ├── useRanking.ts │ ├── useLocalStorage.ts │ └── useResponsive.ts │ └── README.md

phase.name: Phase 1: Foundation
phase.task: Create all type definitions (Contest, Participant, Submission, Activity)
Create 5 Zustand stores with initial state
phase.deliverable: All stores initialized, mock data ready, no UI yet

phase.name: Phase 2: Dashboard
phase.task: Create Layout wrapper (Header, Sidebar, main content)
phase.deliverable: Dashboard page shows all metrics, countdown updates real-time

phase.name: Phase 3: Participants
phase.task: Implement rankingEngine (sort by problems, then penalty)
phase.deliverable: Participant table fully functional with search, sort, filters, pagination

phase.name: Phase 4: Submissions
phase.task: Create VerdictBadge component with color coding
Combine all filters with AND logic
phase.deliverable: Submission table with all filters working independently and combined

phase.name: Phase 5: Leaderboard
phase.task: Create FreezeToggle button with visual indicator
Test: submissions arrive during freeze, rankings don't update
phase.deliverable: Leaderboard with working freeze mode, visual feedback clear

phase.name: Phase 6: Rejudge
phase.task: Make verdict cells clickable in SubmissionRow
Update participant score when verdict changes
phase.deliverable: Rejudge system working end-to-end with all cascades

phase.name: Phase 7: Polish & Deploy
phase.task: Add dark/light mode toggle with persistence
Accessibility audit (ARIA, keyboard nav, color contrast)
phase.deliverable: Deployed, responsive, accessible, ready for interview

keyConcepts.concept: When one action affects multiple stores, coordinate them in the action itself. Example: submissionStore.updateSubmissionVerdict() calls: → participantStore.updateScore() → participantStore.recalculateRankings() (if not frozen) → activityStore.logActivity()
rankingEngine.calculateRankings() and filterEngine.applyFilters() are pure functions. Input → output, no side effects. Easy to test, reuse, reason about.
Use set() to update state: set(state => ({ ...state, newData })) Use getState() to read other store state: useOtherStore.getState().data
Search happens on every keystroke in component state. Real filtering happens 300ms after user stops typing (debounced). Prevents lag on large datasets.
Default CSS is for mobile (320px). Use @media (min-width: 640px) for tablet. Use @media (min-width: 1024px) for desktop. Don't use max-width, always use min-width.
When user clicks freeze: store snapshot of current rankedParticipants. Leaderboard component displays snapshot, not live data. When user unfreezes: clear snapshot, recalculate rankings once.

test.item: Tab navigation works through all elements ✓
Tie in ranking: lower penalty wins ✓

gitCommitSuggestions.commit: setup: initialize next.js with typescript and zustand
types: define contest, participant, submission, activity types
stores: implement all 5 zustand stores with initial state
data: generate realistic mock datasets (150 participants, 500+ submissions)
components/layout: create header, sidebar, main layout wrapper
hooks: implement useContestTimer for real-time countdown
dashboard: build contest overview with stats cards
dashboard: add countdown timer and activity feed
lib/engines: implement ranking engine (sort by problems, penalty)
lib/engines: implement filter engine for participants
hooks: add useDebounce for search optimization
components/participants: build participant table with search, sort, pagination
components/participants: add multi-level filter panel
components/submissions: build submission table with verdict badge
components/submissions: add verdict, problem, participant filtering
components/leaderboard: build leaderboard table with dynamic ranks
components/leaderboard: implement freeze mode toggle and logic
components/submissions: add rejudge modal and verdict click handler
stores: implement rejudge cascade (update score, recalculate rank, log)
components/dashboard: enhance activity feed with icons and timestamps
styles: add mobile-first responsive design (320px to 1440px)
features: add dark/light mode toggle with localStorage persistence
features: add CSV export for leaderboard
a11y: improve accessibility (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, color contrast)
docs: write comprehensive README with setup, architecture, assumptions
chore: final polish and testing
deploy: push to vercel for live deployment

interview.pitch:
I built a Contest Control Center using Next.js and Zustand. The app manages real-time contest monitoring with dynamic leaderboard updates, submission verdict filtering, and complex state cascades.
The architecture has 5 independent Zustand stores—contest, participants, submissions, UI, and activities—that interact through pure functions. When a submission verdict changes, I trigger a ranking recalculation cascade that updates participant scores, recalculates ranks (unless in freeze mode), and logs an activity.
Key features: dynamic leaderboard with freeze mode (submissions log but ranks lock), rejudge system with automatic score/rank updates, multi-level participant filtering (rank + institution + problems solved), responsive mobile-first design, and CSV export.
I shipped it on Vercel with clean architecture focused on maintainability—each component is presentational, state lives in stores, business logic is in pure functions.

answerPoints.point: Separation of concerns: UI, stores, logic engines, data
5 Zustand stores, each with single responsibility
Pure functions for ranking and filtering
Mock data decouples frontend from backend
Data flow example: rejudge → score update → ranking recalculation

question.ask: How does the ranking algorithm work?

answerPoints.point: Primary: problems solved (descending, higher is better)
Secondary: penalty time (ascending, lower is better, tiebreaker)
Algorithm: sort array, assign rank = index + 1
Example: 8 problems, 245 min → Rank 1; 8 problems, 260 min → Rank 2
Called when: submission verdict changes, leaderboard unfreezes
Toggle button locks leaderboard display
During freeze: submissions still logged, but ranking display locked
Snapshot stored when freezing, restored when unfreezing
When unfreezing: rankings recalculated with all submissions
Implementation: conditional recalculateRankings() call

question.ask: How do you handle multi-level filtering?

answerPoints.point: AND logic: all filters must pass
Example: (Rank < 50) AND (Institution = VIT) AND (Problems > 3)
Implementation: filter() with multiple conditions, return false if any fail
Each filter independent, combined in single pass
Pagination resets when filters change

question.ask: Why Zustand over Redux or Context API?

answerPoints.point: Bundle size: 2kb (vs Redux 15kb, Context <1kb)
For 5 stores with ~15 actions each: Zustand is perfect fit
Redux overkill (lots of boilerplate), Context API not scalable
Zustand has clean API, selector-based subscriptions, built-in DevTools
Trade-off: Redux better for 100+ stores (this project is mid-sized)

question.ask: What happens when two stores need to sync?

answerPoints.point: Use getState() to access other stores from within an action
Example: submissionStore calls participantStore.updateScore()
Side effects happen in the action itself
Keep side effects minimal to avoid circular dependencies

question.ask: How do you optimize rendering performance?

answerPoints.point: React.memo for table rows (prevent cascading re-renders)
useMemo for expensive calculations (ranking, filtering)
Pagination: never render more than 50 rows in DOM
Debounced search: 300ms delay prevents filter spam
Selector-based subscriptions in Zustand

question.ask: What would you improve with more time?

answerPoints.point: Unit tests (ranking engine, filter engine, cascades)
Backend integration (real API, database, authentication)
WebSocket for real-time updates
Undo/redo system (Redux DevTools-like)
Advanced features: notifications, analytics, heatmaps
If forced to pick one: unit tests

question.ask: Tell me about a challenging bug you fixed

answerPoints.point: Bug: Ranking not updating after freeze mode
Root cause: participantStore had stale data after submissions came during freeze
Fix: Call syncScoresFromSubmissions() before recalculateRankings()
Lesson: Multi-store apps need explicit sync points

question.ask: What did you learn building this?

answerPoints.point: State management matters: clear patterns prevent bugs
Pure functions are your friend: testable and reusable
Responsive design is a discipline: mobile-first changes your thinking
Separation of concerns: UI shouldn't know about business logic

technicalDeepDives.deepDive: useDebounce hook waits 300ms after user stops typing before filtering. Implementation: useEffect with setTimeout, cleanup function cancels previous timer. Result: Search on "p" → "pr" → "pri" → "priya" = 1 filter operation, not 5.
New activities added to FRONT of array: [newActivity, ...oldActivities] Always slice(0, 10) to show newest 10 events. O(1) insertion, no need to reverse. Max 100 activities stored (old ones deleted).

When two participants have same problems solved: Look at penalty time (minutes from first submission to accepted). Lower penalty wins (they solved it faster). Example: Both solved 5 problems, one in 100 min, one in 120 min → first rank higher.

Rejudge must update all affected stores in order: 1. Submission (verdict) 2. Participant (score) 3. Leaderboard (ranking) 4. Activity (log) If any step fails, entire cascade fails (transaction-like). In real app, would use backend for atomicity.

steps.step: Test production build: npm run start
Push to GitHub: git push origin main

deploymentGuide.readmeTemplate:
# CodeChef Contest Control Center
## Overview A responsive, real-time contest monitoring dashboard for contest organizers.
## Tech Stack - **Frontend**: React.js 18, Next.js 14, TypeScript 5 - **State Management**: Zustand 4.4 - **Styling**: CSS Modules (mobile-first) - **Data**: Mock layer (extensible to API) - **Deployment**: Vercel
## Features - [x] Contest Overview Dashboard (stats, countdown, activities) - [x] Participant Management (search, sort, filter, pagination) - [x] Submission Monitoring (verdict filtering, rejudge) - [x] Dynamic Leaderboard (real-time ranking) - [x] Leaderboard Freeze Mode (lock rankings, continue logging) - [x] Rejudge System (verdict changes with cascade) - [x] Activity Feed (reverse chronological log) - [x] Responsive Design (mobile-first, 320px-1440px+) - [x] Dark/Light Mode (with persistence) - [x] CSV Export (leaderboard data)
## Architecture
### Stores (Zustand) - **contestStore**: Contest metadata, countdown, metrics - **participantStore**: Participants, ranking, filtering, pagination - **submissionStore**: Submissions, verdict filtering - **uiStore**: Theme, freeze mode, active tab - **activityStore**: Activity log (max 100, reverse chronological)
### Business Logic (Pure Functions) - **rankingEngine**: Sort by problems (desc), penalty (asc) - **filterEngine**: Apply multi-level AND filters - **rejudgeManager**: Handle verdict changes and cascades
### Data Flow User Action → Store Action → Pure Function → Cascade → UI Update
## Folder Structure ``` src/ ├── pages/ # Next.js routes ├── components/ # React components (presentational) ├── stores/ # Zustand state management ├── lib/engines/ # Business logic (pure functions) ├── lib/types/ # TypeScript definitions ├── data/mock/ # Mock data (150 participants, 500+ submissions) ├── styles/ # CSS Modules └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ```
## Getting Started
### Prerequisites - Node.js 18+ - npm or yarn
### Installation ```bash git clone <repo> cd codechef-control-center npm install ```
### Development ```bash npm run dev # Open http://localhost:3000 ```
### Build & Deploy ```bash npm run build npm run start npm run deploy # to Vercel (auto-deploy on git push) ```
## Key Design Decisions
### 1. Zustand over Redux - Lightweight (2kb vs 15kb) - Simple API for mid-sized project (5 stores) - Built-in DevTools, selector-based subscriptions - Trade-off: Redux better for 100+ stores
### 2. Pure Function Engines - rankingEngine and filterEngine are pure functions - Input → Output, no side effects - Easy to test, reuse, reason about - Separate from UI and state management
### 3. Freeze Mode Implementation - Store snapshot of rankings when freezing - Skip recalculateRankings() during freeze - Recalculate once when unfreezing - Submissions still logged throughout
### 4. Mobile-First Responsive - Default CSS for 320px (mobile) - Breakpoints: 640px (tablet), 1024px (desktop) - Cards on mobile, grids on tablet, tables on desktop - CSS Modules for scoped styling
### 5. Mock Data Layer - 150 realistic participants from various institutions - 500+ submissions with realistic verdict distribution (70% AC, 20% WA, 5% TLE, 5% RE) - Decoupled from backend—swap with real API later
## State Management Pattern
### Store Action Example ```typescript // Update submission verdict submissionStore.updateSubmissionVerdict(submissionId, newVerdict) ↓ // Trigger cascades participantStore.updateParticipantScore(...) if (!isFrozen) participantStore.recalculateRankings() activityStore.logActivity(...) ↓ // Components subscribed to stores re-render ```
### Multi-Level Filtering ```typescript // AND logic: all filters must pass applyParticipantFilters(participants, { searchTerm: 'priya', selectedInstitution: 'VIT', minRank: 1, maxRank: 50, }) // Returns only: name contains "priya" AND institution = "VIT" AND rank in [1, 50] ```
## Performance Optimizations - **React.memo**: Table rows don't re-render on parent update - **useMemo**: Expensive calculations cached - **Debounced search**: 300ms delay prevents spam - **Pagination**: Max 50 rows in DOM - **Selector subscriptions**: Only necessary components re-render
## Accessibility - WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 color contrast) - Semantic HTML (<table>, <nav>, <main>) - ARIA labels on all buttons and form inputs - Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Escape) - Focus states visible
## Responsive Breakpoints - **Mobile**: 320px - 640px (cards, single column, hamburger menu) - **Tablet**: 640px - 1024px (2-column grid, scrollable tables) - **Desktop**: 1024px+ (full layout, multi-column, fixed sidebar)
## Testing Checklist - [ ] Countdown updates every second - [ ] Search debounces, doesn't lag - [ ] Filters combine with AND logic - [ ] Leaderboard freezes (submissions log, ranks lock) - [ ] Rejudge updates participant score and ranking - [ ] Mobile (375px): no horizontal scroll - [ ] Dark mode: sufficient color contrast - [ ] Keyboard: Tab through all elements
## Assumptions - Single contest (start + end time) - Penalty time is cumulative minutes from first submission to accepted - Ranking tiebreaker: same problems → lower penalty wins - Mock data sufficient for demo (150 participants, 500+ submissions)
## Live Deployment **URL**: [https://your-repo.vercel.app](link)
Deployed on Vercel (auto-deploys on `git push` to main).
## Contact Built by: [Your Name] GitHub: [Your Repo Link]

quickReference.section: I built a Contest Control Center using Next.js and Zustand. The app manages real-time contest monitoring with dynamic leaderboard updates, submission verdict filtering, and complex state cascades. Architecture: 5 Zustand stores + pure function engines. Key feature: rejudge cascade that updates scores, recalculates ranks (unless frozen), and logs activity. Shipped on Vercel with responsive, accessible design.
Sort by: problems (desc) → penalty (asc) Example: 8 problems, 245 min → Rank 1; 8 problems, 260 min → Rank 2
If freezing: frozenSnapshot = [...rankedParticipants] If unfreezing: clear snapshot, call recalculateRankings() During freeze: submissions logged, but leaderboard displays snapshot (locked)
Component state updates on keystroke. useDebounce waits 300ms. setFilters() called after delay.

- Separation of concerns (UI, stores, logic) - 5 Zustand stores, pure function engines - Ranking: problems desc, penalty asc - Freeze mode: snapshot + conditional recalc - Rejudge cascade: 4-step update process - Mobile-first responsive design - Zustand chosen for bundle size + simplicity
✓ Countdown updates ✓ Search doesn't lag ✓ Filters AND together ✓ Freeze locks ranks ✓ Rejudge cascades ✓ Mobile responsive ✓ Keyboard navigation
